title: ImpersonationSession
description: One row per act-as session started by a PlatformUser (Academorix staff) against a tenant User for support / debug purposes. Cross-plane FK — `impersonator_platform_user_id` references `platform_users.id` (central-plane row); every other FK is tenant-plane. The `session_token_hash` is the SHA-256 of the Sanctum PAT minted by identity/auth's `IssueImpersonationPatAction` — the raw token exists only in the impersonator's browser + is returned once at start-time; the hash exists only for lookup + revocation (never surfaced to consumers). Snapshot columns (`impersonator_display_name` + `impersonator_department`) capture the impersonator's identity at session start and are IMMUTABLE thereafter, so compliance queries survive the impersonator's PlatformUser hard-delete (post 90-day retention hold — see platform-user/retention). NEVER soft-deleted (append-only compliance evidence); `session_token_hash` is NULLED after 30 days by `PurgeImpersonationSessionsJob` (the token is expired anyway, but hash scrubbing avoids leaving a residue that could be brute-forced if the hashing algorithm is compromised).

x-eloquent.traits_notes: NO SoftDeletes — impersonation session rows are append-only compliance evidence. NO HasActivityLog because the impersonation lifecycle uses its own dedicated heavy-audit trail (HasAudit) preserved 7 years hot per retention.json; the activity-log substrate is for lighter operational logs.
x-eloquent.relations_note: impersonator() belongsTo PlatformUser via impersonator_platform_user_id (nullable — SET NULL on PlatformUser hard-delete; the display_name + department snapshots preserve identity in the row afterwards). impersonated() belongsTo User via impersonated_user_id. targetTenant() belongsTo Tenant via target_tenant_id. targetApplication() belongsTo Application via target_application_id. The `actor_line` accessor renders 'X (Y department)' from live PlatformUser join OR from snapshot fields if the FK is NULL — always a human-readable actor string for the audit UI.

impersonator_platform_user_id.description: PlatformUser (Academorix staff) initiating the session. NULLABLE with SET NULL on delete so the row survives the PlatformUser's hard-delete at the end of the 90-day retention hold (see platform-user/retention.json). Snapshot columns `impersonator_display_name` + `impersonator_department` preserve identity for compliance queries after this FK NULLs.

impersonated_user_id.description: Tenant User being impersonated. CASCADE on delete — if the target User is erased (GDPR Art. 17), the impersonation record cascades. Impersonator's actions remain in the audit trail (retained per Art. 17(3)(e) legal claims defence).

target_application_id.description: Which Academorix Application the session operates within (Sports, Marketplace, ...). Restrict on delete so an application cannot be retired while impersonation records reference it. A PlatformUser starting a session must specify target_application_id explicitly — the JWT `app` claim on the minted PAT binds to this value.

target_tenant_id.description: The tenant being operated on. CASCADE on tenant erasure — impersonation records die with their tenant (audit trail preserved separately via HasAudit's dedicated table).

session_token_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the Sanctum PAT minted by identity/auth's IssueImpersonationPatAction. NULLABLE — NULLED by PurgeImpersonationSessionsJob 30 days after session end (the token is expired anyway; scrubbing removes hash residue). Never surfaced on the wire (`hidden` in x-eloquent).

reason.description: Required (min 40 chars — heavier than delegation's 20 per rules.json `impersonation_reason_min_length`). Audited. Support-case reason cited by the operator at session start.

support_ticket_reference.description: External ticket / issue ID (Zendesk, Linear, JIRA, GitHub Issues, ...). Optional — some jurisdictions require correlation with a ticket by contract; captured for those tenants.

started_at.description: Session start timestamp. IMMUTABLE — the ImpersonationSessionObserver::updating hook refuses any write to this column. Session TTL is measured from here.

ends_at.description: Hard-cap end. Default = started_at + 60 minutes; may be extended by super_admin (rare, requires `impersonation_extended_ttl` entitlement — enterprise contract-negotiated only). `EndImpersonationSessionsJob` transitions rows past this bound to ended state every 5 minutes; the `impersonation.enforce_active_session` middleware refuses PAT use once now >= ends_at within the same request cycle.

ended_at.description: Actual session end. NULL while the session is active. May be earlier than ends_at (self-service end, force-terminate) or equal to ends_at (ttl_expired).

end_trigger.description: What ended the session. NULL while active. `self_service` = operator hit /end. `ttl_expired` = EndImpersonationSessionsJob ran past ends_at. `admin_revocation` = super_admin fired /force-terminate. `security_lockdown` = security team fired forced-terminate (fires ImpersonationSessionForcedTerminate event + notifies security channel).

is_read_only.description: When true, the `impersonation.enforce_read_only` middleware refuses every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE during the session. Enterprise entitlement `read_only_impersonation` gates whether this can be set true; some tenant contracts require read-only-only impersonation.

impersonator_display_name.description: Snapshot of the impersonator's PlatformProfile display_name (or first + last name if display_name null) at session start. IMMUTABLE after creation. Preserves identity in the row after the impersonator's PlatformUser row hard-deletes (post 90-day retention hold).

impersonator_department.description: Snapshot of PlatformUser.department at session start. IMMUTABLE. Preserves department identity for compliance queries after PlatformUser hard-delete (see platform-user/data/departments.json for the closed enum).

metadata.description: From HasMetadata. `metadata.compliance_regime` is a hook for tenant-specific additional-audit requirements when a healthcare tenant adopts (HIPAA-adjacent hook). Free-form; internal-tier.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_token_hash_unique
indexes.description: Primary read path — the `impersonation.enforce_active_session` middleware hashes the incoming PAT and looks up here. Unique + partial so NULLed rows (post 30-day scrub) don't collide.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_tenant_started_idx
indexes.description: Powers the compliance audit query 'who was impersonated in tenant X between date A and B'. This is THE primary read path for SOC 2 evidence review.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_operator_idx
indexes.description: Powers 'what did operator Y impersonate over time' — the `HasImpersonationHistory` trait's relation on PlatformUser.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_target_idx
indexes.description: Powers the affected-user's retrospective view + 'was this User impersonated between A and B' compliance queries.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_expiry_scan_idx
indexes.description: Powers the 5-minute `EndImpersonationSessionsJob` sweep — finds active sessions past ends_at.

indexes.name: impersonation_sessions_scrub_scan_idx
indexes.predicate: session_token_hash IS NOT NULL AND ended_at IS NOT NULL
indexes.description: Powers the weekly `PurgeImpersonationSessionsJob` — finds ended sessions > 30 days old still carrying a token_hash.

is_active.description: Computed. `ended_at IS NULL AND ends_at > now()`.

is_ended.description: Computed. `ended_at IS NOT NULL OR ends_at <= now()`.

duration_seconds.description: Computed. `(ended_at - started_at)` in seconds; null while active.

remaining_seconds.description: Computed. `(ends_at - now())` for active sessions; null when ended.

was_terminated_early.description: Computed. `end_trigger IN (self_service, admin_revocation, security_lockdown)` (i.e. not ttl_expired).

actor_line.description: Computed. Human-readable operator string, e.g. `Sam Reynolds (support)`. Falls back to snapshot fields when impersonator_platform_user_id is NULL after PlatformUser hard-delete.
